title: dactylorhiza traunsteineri subsp. irenica
intro: Dactylorhiza traunsteineri subsp. irenica, commonly known as the Irenic Dactylorhiza, is a rare subspecies of orchid native to the Balkan Peninsula. As a medicinal plant, Dactylorhiza traunsteineri subsp. irenica has been traditionally used for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, making it useful in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating congestion, and soothing irritated mucous membranes, which may contribute to its use in treating respiratory infections and allergies. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves are typically dried and then used in teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, although the safety and efficacy of these preparations have not been extensively scientifically studied.
